frontier.hypothesis: The g3-basin residual (masked=6, pure v1<->a0 register exchange) may admit a novel closing lever via `register` pin manipulations that were s1/WIP-KILLED on h5-basin but never re-measured on g3-basin under the 2026-07-06 do-while(0)-sanction. Specifically: on g3 base (t0<<=2; arg5=*(s32*)(v0+tbl); t0<<=2; call(...,*(s32*)(t0+tbl_125c),arg5) - no *=4 mult-expander; masked=6 baseline order-perfect), wrap ONLY the arg5 dereference statement in do{}while(0). g3's order-perfect status means wrap-based ref-reweighting doesn't have to compete with a pair-swap residual - only with the v1/a0 register exchange.
frontier.mechanism: s5's F3/F3b/F4 do-while(0) wraps were all measured on h5 base and were monotonically wrong-direction because the wrap's ref-reweighting had to overcome the LAUNCH-generated pair-swap AND lift arg5>t0 simultaneously. On g3 base, LAUNCH is not the residual (the SLLs are non-LAUNCH via multi-set p101 in-place respell); only the qty birth-order/refs differential feeds v1/a0 exchange. Wrap around ONLY arg5's dereference lifts arg5-qty refs*loop-depth without touching t0-qty (which sits outside the wrap), giving a clean pri differential without the pair-swap coupling.
frontier.next_probe: Structural modality: apply g3 base to src/system.c inline block; wrap ONLY `arg5 = *(s32*)(v0 + (s32)tbl_125c);` in `do { ... } while (0)`. Sandbox --disable all. If masked drops below 6, wrap the arg5 chain more aggressively. If INERT at 6, wrap-based ref-lift on g3 is closed too. Save the tested form to rejected/ if KILLED; keep the g3-wrap as a candidate.c anchor if it drops the floor.
